issues: 1x interior detail fully lost — only card silhouette survives
light-bg contrast moderate; border merges slightly with pale surroundings
interior layout feels dense/cluttered — sticker read requires squinting at 4x
reasoning: The card border is genuinely charming — warm golden-brown pixel-art wood with round gem corners hits the 'friendly toy' register nicely and has clear game personality. Silhouette of the card shape itself reads cleanly at 1x as a rectangular UI panel, which is the minimum. However all internal content (bar, pencil graphic, tiny character icons) completely dissolves at 1x; only the colored border survives. On dark bg the card pops well — warm gold against dark reads with authority. On the light bg the border contrast drops noticeably and the card edge is harder to parse. The interior charm is there (cute little pixel characters, pink/blue icons, green areas) but the composition is busy and no single element is instantly iconic at small size. Passes charm and dark-bg criteria; falls short on 1x interior readability and light-bg edge clarity.
